commit.message:
Fix crash when context length exceeds KV cache bounds

Add bounds checking in generate() to prevent out-of-bounds writes to KV
cache when prompt + generation tokens exceed max_kv_len. This fixes CUDA
illegal memory access errors that occurred during long conversations or
concurrent usage.

Harden security, wire circuit breaker, and update roadmap

- Configurable circuit breaker: BackendPool accepts failure_threshold and recovery_time from config
- Health checker respects recovery windows for unhealthy backends
- API key auth middleware with constant-time comparison for admin routes
- Proxy hardening: 10MB body cap, query string preservation, method/header bridging (http 0.2 ↔ 1.x)
- Conditional route registration: admin API off by default, requires api_key when enabled
- Analytics race fix: Arc<Mutex<()>> + open-per-write instead of Arc<Mutex<File>>
- CLI backend spec parser (name=host:port:priority format)
- parse_duration utility for human-readable config values
- Updated roadmap to reflect open-source focus

commit.message:
Fix 0 credits after unauthed purchase + account creation

Root cause: Purchases.logIn(userId) creates a fresh RC user with no
entitlements. The anonymous user's subscription stays on the anonymous
RC ID, so syncSubscriptionToSupabase saw empty entitlements and set
subscription_status='free', never triggering the credit-grant DB trigger.

Fix 1 (revenuecat_service.dart): after logIn(), call restorePurchases()
instead of getCustomerInfo(). restorePurchases() re-validates the device
StoreKit receipt and transfers the anonymous purchase to the identified
RC user, so subsequent getCustomerInfo() shows the active entitlement.

Fix 2 (welcome_free_analysis_page.dart): explicitly clear the credit
service cache before getCreditBalance() so we always fetch fresh data
from Supabase and never show a stale 0-credit value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Also strip truenas metapackage from rootfs install

truenas depends on middlewared which depends on iscsi-scst/scst/scstadmin.
Since we excluded scst from the build, the truenas metapackage can't be
installed. The NVIDIA extension only needs kernel + rootfs, not middlewared.

feat: add `source` heredoc command for inline DSL in CLI scripts

Adds `source [-s] <<DELIM ... DELIM` to the CLI runner, enabling
inline DSL source definitions in .ttrpg-cli scripts. This unblocks
conversion of synthetic integration tests (return, optional_groups,
emit) that construct DSL programs as inline strings.

- `source <<END ... END`: full program (must include system block)
- `source -s <<END ... END`: snippet mode (auto-wraps in system block)
- Unclosed heredocs detected at end of input with clear error message
- Includes 10 unit tests covering basic, snippet, multiline, error cases

research_watch.py writes research/pending-issues.json after each run

finding autonomously, creating a branch for PR review.

PRs require human review before merge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(phase-2): fix toggle button clipping by moving to portal pattern

The circular view-toggle button was inside <aside> which has
overflow:hidden, causing the left half to be clipped and the right
half to crowd panel content.

Fix: move button outside <aside> as a fixed-position portal sibling
(same pattern as AI+ dropdowns). Position tracks panel width via
right:382px (CRM) / right:902px (AI) with right 300ms ease transition
so it animates in sync with the panel width change.

Changes:
- CSS v21→v22: position:fixed + .visible/.ai-view classes + right
  transition + responsive breakpoints + hidden on ≤768px
- JS v18→v19: addClass/removeClass .visible on open/close;
  addClass/removeClass .ai-view in switchToAiView/switchToCrmView
- HTML (both files): button moved from inside <aside> to sibling portal

Also adds "Toggle view" tooltip via CSS ::after pseudo-element
(400ms hover delay, appears left of button).

commit.message:
feat: Add automatic model cascade for rate-limit recovery (v2.6.0)

GeminiImageClient now automatically advances through Nano Banana 2 →
Nano Banana Pro → Nano Banana when quota/rate-limit errors occur,
cycling back after a full round (with 5 s pause) to allow the primary
model's quota to recover.  Fully transparent to all callers.

Key changes:
- gemini_client.py: cascade while-loop in generate_image(), new
  _is_rate_limit_error(), _advance_model_cascade(), get_cascade_status()
  methods; model_cascade constructor parameter; _detect_capabilities()
  re-run on every switch; aspect ratio gracefully downgraded if new
  model lacks extended-ratio support
- model_configs.py: add gemini-2.5-flash-preview-image-generation
  ModelProfile; QUOTA_CASCADE constant documents cascade order
- Version bumped to 2.6.0 across __init__.py, __version__.py,
  pyproject.toml, setup.py, User-Agent headers
- CHANGELOG, README, docs/GEMINI_3_PRO_IMAGE.md all updated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: roll call — all agents respond to 'who is here'

Triggers on: 'who is here', 'who's here', 'who is present', 'roll call', etc.

Steward responds first with a full room summary: all permanent residents,
Scribe status (active or standing by with /scribe hint), and enclave member
list with names fetched live from the DB.

Then each agent checks in, in character, staggered ~1.5s apart:
The Steward → The Architect → The Spark → The Advocate → The Contrarian
→ The Scribe (if active)

Socra is listed in the Steward's summary as a permanent resident but does
not post — it has no chat route, only the wisdom scroll UI.

commit.message:
Add scaling benchmark for tiered storage baseline

Measures performance degradation from "fits in cache" to "exceeds RAM"
across KV, JSON, Vector, and Graph tiers at scales from 1K to 1B records.

Tracks ops/sec, latency percentiles, RSS, disk usage, page faults,
I/O bytes, CPU time, WAL counters, space amplification, and load
throughput curve (first vs last batch) at each scale level.

Add pp2: Spherical Human Packing ("consider a spherical cow...")

New project assuming humans are spheres, in the grand tradition
of physics approximations. Features 6 sphere radius models
(volume-equivalent, shoulder-width, waist-circumference,
height-bounding hamster ball, RMS, BMI-adjusted meatball),
FCC and simple cubic lattice packing, Kepler limit analysis,
and comparison showing spherical humans pack 1.2-2.4x more
than articulated ones across all venues.

When invoices are generated (batch download, individual send, or
contractor batch send), the PDF is now uploaded to Vercel Blob and
saved as a Document record (type: final_invoice) on each claim
included in the invoice. Documents appear automatically in the
claim detail page's Documents section.

commit.message:
Add living design system; consolidate CSS into shared styles.css

Built design-system.html as a living reference that imports the same
production CSS — 13 component cards with live demos, token tables,
color swatches with Figma data attributes, and status lifecycle badges.

Consolidated ~110 lines of duplicated component CSS from candidate.html
and process-log.html into styles.css. Extracted shared layout (sidebar,
mobile nav, header) and all reusable components into the shared sheet.
Created main.js for shared Amplitude init, Session Replay, and mobile
nav logic. Added 11 --chart-* CSS custom properties and a CHART_COLORS
JS constant as single sources of truth for chart colors. Unified
duplicate .overspend-note/.ds-overlap-note into .callout. Updated
CLAUDE.md with CSS consolidation principles and chart color patterns.

commit.message:
refactor(tests): replace fixed waits with dynamic event polling in SuggestionManager tests (#275)

* refactor(tests): replace fixed waits with dynamic event polling in SuggestionManager tests

Replace brittle `await wait(Xms)` calls with `waitForNextCall()` and `waitFor()` helpers
that poll for observable state changes instead of sleeping a fixed duration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve lint and format issues in SuggestionManager test helpers

Add curly braces to single-line if statements (eslint curly rule) and
auto-format with prettier. Also exclude .claude/ from prettier checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(tests): replace all remaining fixed waits with fake timers

Add withFakeTimers() helper that wraps jest.useFakeTimers() +
advanceTimersByTime() + useRealTimers() for negative assertion tests
(asserting getPrediction is NOT called). Remove the now-unused wait()
helper. Suite runs in ~6.8s vs ~9.6s before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: cancel pending restart timer on disable to prevent stale re-enables

When `restart()` schedules a `setTimeout(..., 0)`, calling `disable()` (e.g.
via `destroy()`) did not cancel that timer. The leaked timer could fire during
the next test's async module import, inserting a stale SuggestionManager
instance into the shared harness and causing intermittent test failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Major performance boost: parallelize data loading + auto-analyze on API key entry

Performance improvements (~4-6 second faster initial load):
- Parallelize lab panel fetching: 214 serial fetches → Promise.all()
- Cache loadAllLabs() results so ChartReview doesn't re-fetch
- Parallelize note content loading in longitudinal builder
- Defer AI longitudinal doc init so chart renders immediately
  instead of blocking on AI context assembly

UX improvement:
- Auto-trigger AI analysis as soon as user enters their API key,
  so results are ready by the time they open the AI panel

commit.message:
Feat: knowledgebase changes (#30)

* fixed connection issues edge cases

* chore: prepare for PR

* feat: tag registry + knowledge template bugfix

- Fix YAML tags in knowledge templates (string → array) so frontmatter
  tags are actually parsed instead of silently dropped
- Harden frontmatter parser to accept bare string tags (splits on
  comma/whitespace)
- Add tag registry: instance-level + project-level curated tags merged
  with computed used tags across all boards
- New GET/PUT /api/tags endpoints for tag discovery and management
- New list_tags MCP tool so AI agents discover existing tags before
  inventing new ones
- Update MCP tool descriptions to reference list_tags
- Add project tag management UI in sidebar context menu
- Extend useKnownLabels selector to include project curated tags

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address 7 code review findings for tag system

- Cache computeUsedTags with 5s TTL to avoid O(n*m) on every call
- Update inline tag regex to support unicode ([\p{L}\p{N}_-]+)
- Remove redundant (t: string) casts in context.ts and tools.ts
- Document tag string splitting behavior in frontmatter-parser
- Add error handling + rollback for project tag sync in sidebar
- Extract shared normalizeTag() utility to eliminate duplication
- Verify all templates use consistent YAML array format for tags

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
